description: Establish the Identity Service data layer and REST API scaffolding to support user identity, role management, and the authentication endpoints required by downstream services. This epic covers the normalized schema for users, roles, and session management, JPA entity mapping, Spring Data repositories, and a structured controller layer ready for JWT security integration in Sprint 3. Aligns with Sprint 2 objectives: JPA fundamentals, normalized relational schema design, and REST API scaffolding.
functionality: manage user identity and access roles through a secure, structured API layer
benefit: all downstream services and the frontend can authenticate users and enforce role-based access control consistently

stories.summary: Define identity schema with Flyway migrations
stories.description: Create the normalized relational schema for the Identity Service using Flyway migrations. Covers users, roles, user_roles (many-to-many join table), and refresh_tokens tables as defined in the database schema documentation. Includes indexes and constraints per the schema spec.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Identity Service starts with a clean or versioned database
acceptanceCriteria.then: users, roles, user_roles, and refresh_tokens tables are created with correct columns, constraints, and indexes

acceptanceCriteria.when: a duplicate username or email is inserted
acceptanceCriteria.then: a unique constraint violation is returned

acceptanceCriteria.when: a user is assigned multiple roles
acceptanceCriteria.then: each assignment is persisted as a separate row with user_id and role_id

tasks.summary: Create V1 Flyway migration for users table
tasks.description: Write Flyway migration script to create the users table with user_id, username, email, password_hash, first_name, last_name, status, created_at, updated_at. Add unique indexes on username and email and an index on status.

tasks.summary: Create V2 Flyway migration for roles and user_roles tables
tasks.description: Write Flyway migration to create roles (role_id, role_code, role_name, description) with unique index on role_code, and user_roles (user_id, role_id, assigned_at) with composite primary key and foreign keys referencing users and roles.

tasks.summary: Create V3 Flyway migration for refresh_tokens table
tasks.description: Write Flyway migration to create refresh_tokens (refresh_token_id, user_id, token_value, expires_at, revoked, created_at). Add unique index on token_value and indexes on user_id and expires_at.
Write a Flyway migration to insert the three base roles: APPLICANT, LOAN_OFFICER, ADMIN into the roles table.

stories.summary: Implement JPA entities and Spring Data repositories for Identity Service
stories.description: Map the identity schema to JPA entities using Spring Data JPA. Implement User, Role, UserRole, and RefreshToken entities with correct relationships and create Spring Data repositories for each with custom query methods required by the auth workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: User entity persists and retrieves correctly
acceptanceCriteria.given: a User entity is created with valid fields
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user is persisted and can be retrieved by user_id, username, or email

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user has been assigned roles via user_roles
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user is fetched with roles
acceptanceCriteria.then: the associated Role entities are returned as a collection on the User

acceptanceCriteria.given: a refresh token record is created for a user
acceptanceCriteria.then: the matching RefreshToken entity is returned

tasks.description: Define User entity mapped to users table. Map all columns as fields. Map many-to-many relationship to Role via user_roles join table using @ManyToMany and @JoinTable.
Define Role entity mapped to roles table with role_id, role_code, role_name, description fields.
Define RefreshToken entity mapped to refresh_tokens table. Include @ManyToOne foreign key reference to User entity.
Create Spring Data JPA repositories. UserRepository: findByUsername, findByEmail. RoleRepository: findByRoleCode. RefreshTokenRepository: findByTokenValue, findAllByUserIdAndRevokedFalse.

stories.summary: Scaffold Identity Service controller and service layer
stories.description: Establish the controller-service-repository layered architecture for the Identity Service. Create the AuthController with endpoint stubs for POST /api/v1/auth/login, POST /api/v1/auth/refresh, and GET /api/v1/auth/me. Implement AuthService and UserService skeletons. Wire DTO classes for all request and response shapes. Placeholder implementations return 501 Not Implemented until JWT security is added in Sprint 3.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the endpoint is reachable and returns a structured response

acceptanceCriteria.given: a login request is submitted without a username or password
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 400 Bad Request is returned with a validation error message

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: User profile response shape matches API spec
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response body includes id, username, fullName, role, and permissions fields

tasks.description: Define LoginRequestDto (username, password) with @NotBlank validation. Define LoginResponseDto (accessToken, tokenType, expiresIn, user object with id, username, role).
Define RefreshTokenRequestDto (refreshToken field with @NotBlank). Define UserProfileDto (id, username, fullName, role, permissions list).
Create AuthController at /api/v1/auth with stub handlers for POST /login, POST /refresh, and GET /me. Apply @Valid to request bodies. Return 501 placeholder responses.

Create AuthService with method signatures for login(LoginRequestDto), refreshToken(String), and getCurrentUser(Long). Create UserService with findByUsername and findById. Wire repositories into services.

tasks.summary: Add global exception handler for identity service
tasks.description: Create @RestControllerAdvice that handles MethodArgumentNotValidException and returns standardized 400 error responses matching the platform error payload shape (timestamp, status, error, message, path).

stories.summary: Configure multi-profile application properties for Identity Service
stories.description: Set up application.yaml and application-dev.yaml for the Identity Service. Configure datasource, JPA, Flyway, and server port 8081 per environment. Ensure H2 in-memory database is usable for default/test profile and MySQL is wired for the dev profile.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts with dev profile against MySQL
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Identity Service is started with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: it connects to MySQL, Flyway runs migrations, and the service is available on port 8081

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts with default profile against H2
acceptanceCriteria.then: H2 in-memory database is used and Flyway applies all schema migrations against H2
status UP is returned with a db component status included

tasks.description: Set server.port=8081, spring.application.name=identity-service. Configure H2 datasource as default, Flyway enabled, and JPA show-sql disabled.
Override datasource URL, username, password, and driver class for MySQL dev environment using environment variable references. Set Flyway validate mode and JPA ddl-auto to validate.

tasks.summary: Verify H2 and MySQL connector dependencies in pom.xml
tasks.description: Confirm pom.xml includes H2 (test scope) and MySQL Connector/J (runtime scope). Add if missing.
